commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof plus maintainer auth-boundary review, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the patch adds no dependencies and keeps credential resolution fail-closed, though the auth diagnostic boundary still needs maintainer review. (sha=564e21bb96661970bf3abc54e40255440a099b4c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extracts a shared Gateway auth diagnostic wrapper and routes `gateway usage-cost` and `gateway call <method>` through it, with commander-flow tests for handled and rethrown SecretRef failures.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +101. Total +151 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main sends `gateway call` and `gateway usage-cost` through raw `callGatewayCli(...)` while only `gateway health` catches reachable auth-unavailable failures; I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof plus maintainer auth-boundary review, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix: show reachable gateway auth diagnostic for remaining gateway CLI calls
target.body:
## Summary

`#92290` taught the gateway CLI to recognise when a gateway call fails *before* the
RPC is dispatched because `gateway.auth.token`/`gateway.auth.password` are configured
as SecretRefs that cannot be resolved in this command path. Instead of leaking a raw
`GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError`, the `health` subcommand now probes the gateway and,
when it is reachable but the CLI simply has no usable credentials, prints an actionable
diagnostic (`Gateway: reachable` + "set OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN/OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD").

That fix only wired `gateway health` (plus `browser doctor` and `channels status`). The
two remaining `callGatewayCli(...)` entrypoints in the **same file** —
`gateway usage-cost` and `gateway call <method>` — resolve credentials through the exact
same path (`callGatewayWithScopes`, independent of the RPC method name), so they fail with
the identical error, yet still surface only the raw `String(err)` from `runGatewayCommand`.

This PR closes that symmetric gap: `usage-cost` and `call` now reuse the same reachable
gateway auth diagnostic as `health`.

## What changed

- `src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts`
  - Extracted the `health` subcommand's catch-template into a shared helper
    `callGatewayCliWithAuthDiagnostic(method, rpcOpts, params)`. On credential-resolution
    failure it runs the existing `emitReachableGatewayAuthDiagnostic(...)`; if the gateway
    is reachable-but-unauthenticated it emits the diagnostic and returns `{ handled: true }`,
    otherwise it rethrows the raw error for the default handler.
  - `gateway usage-cost` and `gateway call <method>` now route through this helper.
  - No behaviour change for the reachable/success paths or for non-auth errors.

## Real behavior proof (real runtime, outside tests)

A standalone `tsx` script (not a vitest file) drives the **real** exported functions across
a **real network boundary**: it starts a real `ws` WebSocket server on a real TCP port, lets
the real `GatewayClient` probe connect to it, and exercises the real
`emitReachableGatewayAuthDiagnostic` with a real `GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError` and a real
config whose `gateway.auth.token` is an unresolvable env SecretRef.

```
$ node --import tsx proof-gateway-auth-diagnostic.mts

== Part A: real emitReachableGatewayAuthDiagnostic over a real WS boundary ==
  --- positive captured output ---
    | Gateway: reachable
    | Gateway is reachable, but this CLI has no token/password or paired device token for read-scope health RPCs.
  [PASS] positive: handled === true -- handled=true
  [PASS] positive: prints 'Gateway: reachable'
  [PASS] positive: prints credentials-required diagnostic
  [PASS] positive: did NOT leak raw GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError
  [PASS] positive: exit(1) requested
  [PASS] negative(dead port): handled === false -- handled=false
  [PASS] negative(dead port): did NOT print reachable diagnostic

== Part B: REAL register.ts wiring -- `gateway usage-cost` emits the diagnostic ==
  registered gateway subcommands: run, status, install, uninstall, start, stop, restart, call, usage-cost, health, stability, diagnostics, probe, discover
  [PASS] wiring: 'usage-cost' subcommand registered
  [PASS] wiring: 'call' subcommand registered
  [PASS] wiring: 'health' subcommand registered (reference)
  [PASS] wiring: usage-cost + call both call callGatewayCliWithAuthDiagnostic -- 2 call sites (usage-cost + call)

ALL PROOF CHECKS PASSED
```

What this proves end to end, against real runtime (no mocks of the function under test):

- **Real WS boundary** — the real `GatewayClient` probe connects to a real `ws` server which
  closes with `1008 "pairing required"`. The probe records `gateway closed (1008): pairing
  required`, which is recognised as reachability evidence (`gatewayProbeResultSawGateway`).
- **Positive** — with the gateway reachable but credentials unresolved, the real
  `emitReachableGatewayAuthDiagnostic` returns `true`, prints `Gateway: reachable` plus the
  credentials-required diagnostic, requests `exit(1)`, and does **not** leak the raw error.
- **Negative control** — pointing the same config at a now-closed (dead) port makes the probe
  see no gateway, so the helper returns `false` and emits no diagnostic — the caller would
  rethrow the raw error, exactly the old behaviour. This is the same `handled === false`
  branch `usage-cost`/`call` rely on to preserve prior output for non-reachable failures.

## Tests and validation

- `src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts` — 4 new tests drive the real commander program
  registered by `registerGatewayCli`:
  - `usage-cost` and `call` each emit the reachable-gateway auth diagnostic (and pass the
    correct `token`/`password`/error) when credential resolution fails and the gateway is
    reachable, with **no** raw error leaked.
  - Negative cases: when the diagnostic is not handled (gateway not reachable), both commands
    rethrow the raw error and print the original `Gateway usage cost failed` / `Gateway call
    failed` message — proving the prior behaviour is preserved.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts` → **24/24 passed**.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` → clean.

## Risk checklist

- [x] Scope limited to two `callGatewayCli` entrypoints in one file (`register.ts`); reuses the
      existing, already-merged `emitReachableGatewayAuthDiagnostic` from `#92290`.
- [x] No behaviour change on success/reachable paths or for non-auth errors (the helper only
      acts when the gateway is reachable-but-unauthenticated, otherwise rethrows).
- [x] Diagnostic text and semantics are unchanged from `#92290`; this PR only widens where it
      applies. It never claims a method "requires auth" — it states the gateway is reachable but
      the CLI has no usable credentials, which is accurate for any pre-dispatch credential failure.
- [x] No new dependencies.
target.author: alix-007
